perturbation.original_text: B. Cash Payment: (1) HDS will provide payment of Fifty Thousand and NO/100 Dollars ($50,000) made payable to Celebrity's agent. CSE, as follows $10,000 Within ten (10) days of the parties execution of this Agreement $15,000 Within 60 days of the parties execution of this agreement $25,000* Ten (10) days prior to the Production Session [*Should Company decide not to conduct the Production Session, then the $25,000 allocated for said Production Session ("Production Fee") will not be owed to Celebrity and notice should be given to CSE as soon as reasonably known by Company if Company does not intend to conduct the Production Session. However, if Company schedules the Production Session, then said Production Fee is non-refundable regardless if the Production Session occurs. ] (a) CSE, shall invoice HDS for these fees and HDS shall pay such invoice within ten (10) business days following receipt of the CSE invoice. (b) Payments shall be made to CSE and delivered to 600 Galleria Parkway, Suite 1900, Atlanta, Georgia 30339.
perturbation.changed_text: B. Cash Payment: (1) HDS will provide payment of Fifty Thousand and NO/100 Dollars ($50,000) made payable to Celebrity's agent. CSE, as follows $10,000 Within ten (10) days of the parties execution of this Agreement $15,000 Within 60 days of the parties execution of this agreement $25,000* Ten (10) days prior to the Production Session [*Should Company decide not to conduct the Production Session, then the $25,000 allocated for said Production Session ("Production Fee") will not be owed to Celebrity and notice should be given to CSE as soon as reasonably known by Company if Company does not intend to conduct the Production Session. However, if Company schedules the Production Session, then said Production Fee is non-refundable regardless if the Production Session occurs. ] (a) CSE, shall invoice HDS for these fees and HDS shall pay such invoice within thirty (30) business days following receipt of the CSE invoice. (b) Payments shall be made to CSE and delivered to 600 Galleria Parkway, Suite 1900, Atlanta, Georgia 30339.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'ten (10) business days' to 'thirty (30) business days' for invoice payment in Section 5.B.(1)(a) creates a contradiction with Section 10.B.(2), which allows Celebrity to terminate the agreement if HDS fails to provide consideration within ten (10) days following the date such consideration is due. This creates uncertainty as to the actual deadline for HDS to make payments after receiving an invoice from CSE, potentially leading to disputes and premature termination claims.
perturbation.contradicted_text: B. Celebrity shall have the right to terminate this Agreement upon ten (10) days prior written notice to HDS in the event of the occurrence of any of the following: (1) HDS adjudicates as insolvent or declares bankruptcy; or (2) HDS fails to provide consideration due pursuant to this Agreement, within ten (10) days following the date such consideration is due hereunder, provided that HDS is notified in writing of such non-payment by Celebrity and such payment by HDS is not made within three (3) days following such notification; or (3) HDS fails breaches any covenant or agreement set forth herein and fails to cure same (if curable) within seven (7) days of receipt of written notice. Furthermore, HDS agrees that such termination shall not relieve it of its obligation to provide consideration as contemplated hereunder. Celebrity shall not have waived any of its rights at law or in equity by exercising any provision of this paragraph.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 5.B.(1)(a) from 'ten (10) business days' to 'thirty (30) business days' for invoice payment. This directly contradicts Section 10.B.(2), which states that Celebrity can terminate the agreement if HDS fails to provide consideration within 'ten (10) days following the date such consideration is due'. This creates a direct inconsistency regarding the payment deadline, and thus creates a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Review, Approval, and Ownership of Advertising. All HDS' uses of Celebrity Attributes in connection with the Commercial and/or press releases shall be subject to the prior written approval of Celebrity via his agent, CSE. Said written approval must be given within five (5) business days of CSE's receipt or said usage shall be deemed unapproved. Any such usage featuring Celebrity in the Commercial and/or press releases shall be and remain the property of HDS; however, HDS shall have the right to use said Commercial and/or press releases solely as outlined in Paragraph 3 and only during the Term. Celebrity may use said materials in whole or in part solely for the purpose of presenting Celebrity's work in Celebrity's personal portfolio, website or otherwise and/or on Celebrity's agent's website. Such usage may not be sold or transferred.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Review, Approval, and Ownership of Advertising. All HDS' uses of Celebrity Attributes in connection with the Commercial and/or press releases shall be subject to the prior written approval of Celebrity via his agent, CSE. Said written approval must be given within fifteen (15) business days of CSE's receipt or said usage shall be deemed unapproved. Any such usage featuring Celebrity in the Commercial and/or press releases shall be and remain the property of HDS; however, HDS shall have the right to use said Commercial and/or press releases solely as outlined in Paragraph 3 and only during the Term. Celebrity may use said materials in whole or in part solely for the purpose of presenting Celebrity's work in Celebrity's personal portfolio, website or otherwise and/or on Celebrity's agent's website. Such usage may not be sold or transferred.
perturbation.explanation: The change in Section 9 from 'five (5) business days' to 'fifteen (15) business days' for approval creates a contradiction with Paragraph 10.A, which allows HDS to terminate the agreement if Celebrity fails to perform duties or breaches any agreement, failing to cure within seven (7) days of written notice. If Celebrity/CSE takes longer than seven days but less than fifteen days to provide approval, it is unclear whether HDS can terminate the agreement, leading to potential disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. HDS shall have the right to terminate this Agreement upon ten (10) days prior written notice to Celebrity in the event Celebrity fails to perform the duties set forth in Paragraph 4 hereof or breaches any other covenant or agreement set forth herein and fails to cure same (if curable) within seven (7) days of receipt of written notice. Such termination shall relieve HDS of its obligation to provide any further consideration pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The modification in Section 9, extending the approval timeframe to fifteen (15) business days, directly contradicts the termination clause in Paragraph 10.A, which allows termination if a breach isn't cured within seven (7) days of written notice. A scenario arises where Celebrity takes between 8 and 15 days to approve, which would be a breach under Paragraph 9 but seemingly not curable within the timeframe allowed by Paragraph 10.A, creating a conflict regarding HDS's right to terminate, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction', where conflicting deadlines are established in different sections.

perturbation.original_text: 10. Termination A. HDS shall have the right to terminate this Agreement upon ten (10) days prior written notice to Celebrity in the event Celebrity fails to perform the duties set forth in Paragraph 4 hereof or breaches any other covenant or agreement set forth herein and fails to cure same (if curable) within seven (7) days of receipt of written notice. Such termination shall relieve HDS of its obligation to provide any further consideration pursuant to this Agreement. B. Celebrity shall have the right to terminate this Agreement upon ten (10) days prior written notice to HDS in the event of the occurrence of any of the following: (1) HDS adjudicates as insolvent or declares bankruptcy; or (2) HDS fails to provide consideration due pursuant to this Agreement, within ten (10) days following the date such consideration is due hereunder, provided that HDS is notified in writing of such non-payment by Celebrity and such payment by HDS is not made within three (3) days following such notification; or (3) HDS fails breaches any covenant or agreement set forth herein and fails to cure same (if curable) within seven (7) days of receipt of written notice. Furthermore, HDS agrees that such termination shall not relieve it of its obligation to provide consideration as contemplated hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. Termination A. HDS shall have the right to terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days prior written notice to Celebrity in the event Celebrity fails to perform the duties set forth in Paragraph 4 hereof or breaches any other covenant or agreement set forth herein and fails to cure same (if curable) within seven (7) days of receipt of written notice. Such termination shall relieve HDS of its obligation to provide any further consideration pursuant to this Agreement. B. Celebrity shall have the right to terminate this Agreement upon ten (10) days prior written notice to HDS in the event of the occurrence of any of the following: (1) HDS adjudicates as insolvent or declares bankruptcy; or (2) HDS fails to provide consideration due pursuant to this Agreement, within ten (10) days following the date such consideration is due hereunder, provided that HDS is notified in writing of such non-payment by Celebrity and such payment by HDS is not made within three (3) days following such notification; or (3) HDS fails breaches any covenant or agreement set forth herein and fails to cure same (if curable) within seven (7) days of receipt of written notice. Furthermore, HDS agrees that such termination shall not relieve it of its obligation to provide consideration as contemplated hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the termination notice period for HDS in Section 10.A from 'ten (10) days' to 'thirty (30) days' creates an in-text contradiction with the termination notice period for Celebrity in Section 10.B, which remains at 'ten (10) days'. This inconsistency creates uncertainty regarding the notice periods required for termination and potential disputes over the legitimacy of a termination depending on which party initiates it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: B. Celebrity shall have the right to terminate this Agreement upon ten (10) days prior written notice to HDS in the event of the occurrence of any of the following: (1) HDS adjudicates as insolvent or declares bankruptcy; or (2) HDS fails to provide consideration due pursuant to this Agreement, within ten (10) days following the date such consideration is due hereunder, provided that HDS is notified in writing of such non-payment by Celebrity and such payment by HDS is not made within three (3) days following such notification; or (3) HDS fails breaches any covenant or agreement set forth herein and fails to cure same (if curable) within seven (7) days of receipt of written notice. Furthermore, HDS agrees that such termination shall not relieve it of its obligation to provide consideration as contemplated hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 10.A stated that HDS could terminate the agreement with ten (10) days' notice. The changed text modifies this to thirty (30) days' notice. Section 10.B, the contradicted text, states that Celebrity can terminate the agreement with ten (10) days' notice. The change creates an inconsistency in the notice periods required for termination, creating a contradiction between the rights and obligations of each party. This falls under the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties.
